free_text: Search for quasimolecules in heavy-ion reactions in the
region of the Coulomb barrier.
Tandem accelerator produced the 14N and 13C ion beams;
The 14N ions using the ion impact method with a Kr beam
from the duoplasmatron and the 13C ions using 13CO gas
in the duoplasmatron with an enrichment of 13C to 95%.
The silicon surface-barrier alpha detectors
were covered with Al masks 8 to 12 mu-m thick,
absorbing all heavy ions occurring. The surface-barrier
p-detectors were also covered with Al masks absorbing
the alpha particles. The linear solid angle of the
detectors was about +-2 adeg.
The heavy ions were detected by a dE-E
telescope.
The self-supporting 10B and 12C targets were about 10
to 15 mu-g/cm2 thick corresponding to an energy loss
of about 40 keV for 12C ions with an energy of 6.5 MeV
